behaviors.description: The generated function signature matches the expected interface (parameter names, types, return type) specified in the description.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP is primary: correctness depends on what the real model generates for interface details, not just deterministic wrapper logic. MOCK-HIDE: a stubbed model can be forced to return a perfect signature and would miss real mis-generation failures. Rejected Workflow: behavior text targets one generation outcome ("generated function signature matches..."), not multiple model decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run live generation for interface-constrained prompts, parse the produced function signature, and assert parameter names/types/return type match the spec.

behaviors.description: Orchestration executes attempts in order and returns immediately on the first passing attempt.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS points to module handoff: orchestrator + attempt runner + result handling. In-process fakes reproduce ordering/early-return bugs, so System is not required. Rejected System by behavior text "executes attempts in order and returns immediately" (control-flow logic, not OS/network/subprocess-specific). Rejected Agentic: this checks orchestration mechanics, not model-output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator to fake generator and fake test executor with scripted pass/fail sequence; assert attempt order and that processing stops immediately after first pass.

behaviors.description: For a function that a single call usually fails, the live iterative loop can still produce a passing implementation within the allowed attempts.
behaviors.justification: MULTI-STEP + LLM-DEP: requires real generate->test->refine iterations where later model calls use prior failure context. A single-step agentic test cannot validate loop-level recovery behavior across attempts. TIE-BREAKER evidence for higher level: "live iterative loop" and "within the allowed attempts" explicitly require sequential agentic steps.
behaviors.test_description: Use a known prompt where first live generation commonly fails; run full live loop up to 3 attempts and assert at least one later attempt passes and is returned.

behaviors.description: When tests fail, the refinement prompt builder includes the failed test names and failure messages in the next LLM prompt.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at module boundary: test-result objects must be handed to prompt builder and then to next-request payload. In-process wiring catches this. Rejected Agentic by behavior text "includes the failed test names and failure messages" (prompt-content plumbing), which does not require judging real model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run one failed attempt with deterministic failure fixtures, capture the next prompt payload, and assert it contains failed test names and failure messages.

behaviors.description: The next live attempt changes implementation content in response to prior failure context rather than resubmitting the same code.
behaviors.justification: Requires at least two agentic steps in sequence (prior failed attempt + next live refinement) and checks context-conditioned model behavior. MOCK-HIDE: deterministic stubs can always change code and miss real non-response-to-feedback failures. Rejected Agentic (single-step) because behavior text says "next live attempt" and "in response to prior failure context," which depends on sequential handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Run live attempt 1 to collect real failures, run live attempt 2 with that context, and assert attempt-2 code differs materially from attempt-1 and reflects addressed failure areas.

behaviors.description: The service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/bookkeeping across modules; in-process fakes can deterministically reproduce 3 failures and validate payload structure. Rejected System: behavior text focuses on "stops after 3" and returned structure, not real OS/network/subprocess semantics. Rejected Agentic: no dependence on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use scripted failing generator/test-runner responses for three attempts; assert loop stops at exactly 3 and error payload includes all attempt artifacts/results.

behaviors.description: The full non-agentic pipeline wiring (generate call, test execution, attempt bookkeeping) works end to end with deterministic stubbed model outputs.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly says "non-agentic" and "deterministic stubbed model outputs," disqualifying Agentic/Workflow. It is still cross-module end-to-end wiring, beyond one function. Rejected System unless test execution specifically requires real subprocess/OS behavior; behavior text does not require that.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end pipeline in-process with deterministic model stubs and deterministic test outcomes; assert call sequence, state transitions, and final bookkeeping are correct.

behaviors.description: The full live loop (generate, test, refine, retest) solves at least one known hard function that fails in the first attempt.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires multiple agentic steps: "generate, test, refine, retest" plus success after initial failure. Single agentic-call tests cannot cover this sequence-level property. TIE-BREAKER evidence for higher level is directly in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real loop on a curated hard prompt where first attempt is expected to fail; verify later attempts in the same run eventually pass within max attempts.
